heroes.note: Score driven by JCI's worst-in-world YTD decline (~34%), rupiah at historic lows near IDR 18,036 (10th straight weekly fall), Moody's Baa2/negative on Danantara, May CPI surprise at 3.08%, and near-vanished April trade surplus of only US$0.09bn.

Persistent foreign outflows, sovereign credit-watch risks, a rupiah near record lows despite a 50bps BI rate hike, and MSCI/FTSE index exclusions leave no near-term catalyst for a sustainable reversal.

news.title: JCI Crashes 4.2% to 5,594 — Worst Single-Day Drop in 2026, 656 Stocks in Red
news.impact: Collapse below 5,600 accelerates forced selling by passive funds and margin calls; next technical support at 5,400 over 1-2 weeks.

news.title: Moody's Assigns Baa2/Negative to Danantara Investment Management, Citing Governance Risks on $900bn BUMN Asset Vehicle
news.impact: Sovereign-aligned negative rating widens equity discount rate and deepens foreign risk-premium on all Indonesian assets over the next month.

news.title: May 2026 CPI Surges to 3.08% YoY, Above 2.97% Consensus and Central Bank Target Midpoint
news.impact: Above-target inflation narrows BI's room to cut rates, sustaining high borrowing costs for leveraged corporates and pressuring consumer-sector earnings over the next quarter.

news.title: Rupiah Hits Fresh Record Low Near IDR 18,036/USD — Asia's Worst-Performing Currency in 2026
news.impact: Sustained rupiah weakness raises imported inflation and dollar-denominated debt burdens for corporates; BI FX intervention further depletes reserves near two-year lows.

news.title: All Commodity Exporters Must Route Through PT Danantara Sumber Daya Indonesia from June 1 — Single-Gate Export Reform Live
news.impact: Policy uncertainty around Permendag implementation is disrupting export FX supply and spooking foreign investors in commodity equities over the next 2-4 weeks.

news.title: FTSE Russell Removes 8 Indonesian Stocks from Index; MSCI Had Already Cut 19 in May Rebalancing
news.impact: Forced passive-fund selling of excluded names adds structural selling pressure and further reduces Indonesia's weight in global EM benchmarks over coming weeks.

news.title: Coal Futures Rise Above $146/ton — Nine-Week High on China Shanxi Mine Blast and Supply Disruptions
news.impact: Higher Newcastle coal prices provide earnings support for ADRO, PTBA, and ITMG over the next 1-2 months, partially cushioning mining-sector valuation pressure.

regulations.sub: Reference price set at USD 1,029.51/MT · Effective Jun 1–30

regulations.title: BI Emergency Rate Hike — BI-Rate to 5.25% (BI Board Resolution May 20, 2026)
regulations.sub: 50bps surprise hike · First hike since April 2024

regulations.title: Bond Stabilisation Fund — Government Plan to Support SBN Market
Nickel Ore Mining Quota Reduction 2026 (ESDM Directive)

calendar.detail: Next BI Board of Governors meeting; markets pricing 25bps hold or further hike
Fed rate decision; higher-for-longer expectations weigh on EM assets
April surplus collapsed to US$0.09bn; May data critical for IDR outlook
Following May's above-consensus 3.08% print; inflation trajectory key for BI
April reserves fell to USD 146.2bn (near 2-year low); May print scrutinised

ratings.action: Negative outlook reaffirmed on banks May 2026

precedents.html: In 1997–1998, the rupiah fell over 80% and the JCI lost more than 65% as twin currency and equity crises fed each other — the current ~10% YTD IDR slide and 34% YTD JCI decline are not yet systemic, but the feedback loop pattern is recognisable.

During May–September 2013 (Taper Tantrum), the rupiah weakened ~18% and the 10-year bond yield surged ~300bps before BI's emergency 175bps of hikes stabilised the currency — today's 50bps hike to 5.25% echoes that defensive playbook but may require further tightening.

When Moody's cut Indonesia's sovereign outlook to negative in 2020, the JCI fell an additional 8% in the following 30 days before stabilising on fiscal support — the current Baa2/negative assigned to Danantara creates a comparable sentiment shock given its US$900bn BUMN asset base.

geo.html: Stalled US–Iran negotiations keep Brent near $95–98/barrel, inflating Indonesia's fuel import bill, widening the trade deficit, and pressuring fiscal accounts — Now: April trade surplus collapsed to just US$0.09bn, the smallest in six years.

A Shanxi, China coal mine explosion has triggered safety inspections and output suspensions across China's largest coal-producing region, tightening global thermal coal supply and lifting Newcastle futures to $146/ton — Now: bullish for IDX coal exporters ADRO, PTBA, ITMG over the near term.

Rising US dollar strength driven by Fed higher-for-longer expectations and EM defensive rate hikes (BI +50bps) is accelerating foreign outflows from Indonesian equities and bonds, with foreign bond holdings near a 20-year low — Now: rupiah at a historic record low of IDR 18,036/USD despite BI intervention draining reserves to USD 146.2bn.
